input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10. Let me provide guidance on this.

In .NET MAUI, the `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated/removed in newer versions. The recommended approach for .NET 10 (and starting from .NET 9 updates) is to use the `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` behavior or handle it differently.

Actually, let me think about this more carefully. 

The issue is that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` relied on Android's `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE`, which was deprecated by Android itself starting with API 30 (Android 11+). MAUI's cross-platform solution is `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll`, which automatically scrolls content when the keyboard appears and was introduced to handle this scenario across platforms.

With Android 15 and .NET 10, edge-to-edge display enforcement means the old resize approach no longer works—you need to handle `WindowInsets` properly instead. The practical solution is ensuring your content lives inside a `ScrollView` so `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` can function correctly, or implementing proper insets handling for Android 15+ specifically.
